channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SANParks Continues Recovery Efforts After Heavy Flooding in Garden Route National Park
item.description: The South African National Parks (SANParks) is actively working with teams and stakeholders to restore access and repair infrastructure across the Garden Route National Park after severe rainfall caus...

item.title: Africa gradually adopts yuan for practical financial and trade reasons
item.description: This article explains that Africa’s increasing use of China’s renminbi (RMB) is less about politics and more about practical financial decisions. While some global observers frame it as a geopolitical...

item.title: APC Officially Approves Governor Yahaya to Contest Gombe North Senate Seat
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) don officially clear Gombe State Governor, Muhammadu Inuwa Yahaya, to contest the Gombe North Senatorial District seat for their upcoming primary elections. The gov...

item.title: Independent candidates receive major donations following High Court ruling on Victoria’s campaign finance laws
item.description: In Victoria, independent candidates Sophie Torney and Shima Ibuki have received substantial donations from Climate 200, a group supporting the teal movement, following a High Court ruling that declare...

item.title: SAWS Issues Warnings for Cold Front Affecting Western and Northern Cape
item.description: The South African Weather Service (SAWS) has issued warnings as a cold front is expected to hit the western parts of the Western Cape and south-western Namakwa in the Northern Cape starting Sunday mor...

item.title: Reform UK Makes Significant Gains as Labour Loses Ground in 2026 Local Elections
item.description: The 2026 local elections across England, Wales and Scotland have seen significant political shifts. Labour has suffered heavy losses, ceding control of 12 councils and losing the Hackney mayoralty to ...

item.title: PSC Scraps Master's Degree Requirement for Kenya Prisons Promotions After Successful Petition by Activists
item.description: Prison officers in Kenya have something to celebrate after the Public Service Commission (PSC) and Kenya Prisons Service agreed to review and change their promotion criteria. This came after a year-lo...

item.title: Labour MPs Debate Keir Starmer's Leadership Following Poor Election Results
item.description: Following a string of disappointing election outcomes, a growing number of Labour MPs are publicly questioning Sir Keir Starmer's leadership. At least a dozen MPs have suggested that he should resign ...

item.title: Nairobi Resident Files Sh200 Million Lawsuit Against Hospital Over Alleged Expired Medication
item.description: A Nairobi man, Dominic Nyang'au, has taken a city hospital to court seeking KSh200 million, claiming he suffered severe health issues after allegedly being given expired drugs. Nyang'au filed the case...

item.title: Oracle Denies Requests for Enhanced Severance Following Mass Layoffs
item.description: In a major workforce reduction on March 31, 2026, Oracle laid off between 20,000 and 30,000 employees via email. Affected workers experienced immediate account deactivation on VPN and Slack, followed ...

item.title: Lawyers Kaufman and Jacobs officially withdraw from representing Duterte
item.description: In a recent development, lawyers Nicholas Kaufman and Jacobs have officially withdrawn from representing former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte. The withdrawal comes amidst ongoing legal and publ...

item.title: Political parties criticise Constitutional Court delay on Phala Phala judgment
item.description: Political parties have voiced frustration over the delay by the Constitutional Court in delivering a ruling on whether the National Assembly failed to hold President Cyril Ramaphosa accountable in the...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation: TVK Receives Left Support, VCK Yet to Decide
item.description: In Tamil Nadu, uncertainty over the new government continued as actor-turned-politician C. Joseph Vijay’s party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), secured unconditional support from the Communist Party ...

item.title: Kenya to Host Africa Forward Summit, Showcasing Youth-Led Digital Innovations
item.description: Kenya is strategically positioning itself as a leader in digital innovation across Africa as it gears up to host the inaugural Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, in partnership with the French governme...

item.title: U.S. Expands Passport Revocations for Parents Owing Child Support
item.description: The U.S. State Department has announced a new policy to revoke passports of Americans with significant overdue child support, marking a shift from previous practices. Previously, only those applying f...

item.title: Local elections 2026 highlight fragmentation of traditional UK politics
item.description: The 2026 local elections in England, Scotland, and Wales have indicated a significant shift away from the traditional two-party system. Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, celebrated gains in London counc...

item.title: Pennsylvania’s Paid Leave Bill May Not Fully Support Sandwich Generation Caregivers
item.description: The number of family caregivers in the U.S. has grown from 53 million in 2020 to 63 million in 2025, and this trend is expected to continue as the baby boomer population ages. Family caregivers, often...

item.title: 'Kuwentong Nanay' on It's Showtime Highlights the Joys and Challenges of Moms This Mother's Day
item.description: This Mother's Day 2026, ABS-CBN's 'It's Showtime' featured a special segment called 'Kuwentong Nanay,' which showcased touching, funny, and at times heartbreaking stories about Filipino mothers. The s...

item.title: Motorcycle dispute in Taguig results in stabbing of passenger
item.description: In Taguig City, Metro Manila, a road rage incident turned violent on Thursday when a motorcycle rider, identified only as 48-year-old Sherwin, stabbed the back-rider of another motorcycle using a scre...

item.title: Mozambican convicted and sentenced to multiple life terms for murder of Free State police officer
item.description: A Mozambican man, Frimino David Matola, 55, has been sentenced to multiple life terms by the Free State High Court for the murder of Sgt Disema Steven Mapane during a shoot-out in Welkom. Matola was a...
